colors.name: washi
colors.role: background — the aged paper the whole ceremony is burned onto; all contrast figures are measured against it

colors.name: washi-warm
colors.role: the page's brighter skin — hero and climax ground, a half-step lighter than the body paper so the bowls feel lit (surface only)

colors.name: tokonoma
colors.role: the alcove — the slightly darker, recessed paper of the tokonoma (床の間) where the scroll and ikebana hang; ink reads 9.96:1 on it

colors.name: sumi
colors.role: the ink: primary text, the wordmark, the bowl outline, ceremony headers — never pure black, wabi-sabi keeps it warm (11.61:1 on washi)

colors.name: ink-soft
colors.role: sumi thinned with water — secondary text: English annotations, beat labels, captions, the colophon ledger (4.58:1)

colors.name: rule
colors.role: the hairline — page rules, the tokonoma frame, beat dividers; 1.4:1 so large/decorative lines only, never text

colors.name: matcha
colors.role: the one living accent: the chasen whisk strokes, the bowl's tea surface, the seal, hairlines of emphasis, ::selection ground, focus ring. 4.48:1 on washi — decorative and large only, never body text

colors.name: matcha-deep
colors.role: matcha that must function as text on paper — the bowl's romanized name, small matcha labels, the few words that earn the accent at body size (6.10:1, AA). Used wherever matcha would otherwise drop below 4.5:1

fonts.role: every CJK glyph — the tategaki ceremony headers (茶道, 一期一会), the alcove scroll, beat labels, the bowl's name. A mincho whose vertical metrics were drawn first; it carries writing-mode: vertical-rl with no complaint. Kanji fall through Cormorant into it with no markup
the Latin voice — the wordmark CHADŌ, English annotations, the colophon, romanized breath beats. A quiet noble serif with a long ascender, in italic for the second-language answers

texture: Washi in two static layers, both fixed and never animated: (1) one full-viewport SVG feTurbulence pass — fractalNoise baseFrequency 0.62, 2 octaves, seed 17 — mapped by feColorMatrix to sumi at ≤4.5% alpha and multiplied over everything (aged paper fibers, not film grain); (2) a warm age-vignette, radial-gradient to rgba(91,80,58,0.06) at the edges, so the page reads as paper that has yellowed evenly in a drawer for decades. The tokonoma alcove adds its own paper: a tokonoma ground one step darker than the body, a 1px sumi-hairline frame rebated 2.5vw in, and a faint second feTurbulence (baseFrequency 0.04, 2 octaves) only inside the alcove, so the recess reads as deeper, older paper. No grain animates — stillness is the material.

motion.name: ease-tea
motion.value: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) — every entrance: rises soft, settles long, like steam leaving a kettle

motion.name: ease-bow
motion.value: cubic-bezier(0.65, 0, 0.35, 1) — the breath cue and bowl fill: weighted, deliberate, two-handed

motion.name: breath-cycle
motion.value: inhale 4s (ease-out, scale 0.92→1) · hold 4s (still) · exhale 4s (ease-in, scale 1→0.92) · rest 2s — a 14-second bow, the pace of someone breathing opposite the kettle. Drives the hero cue; gate labels cross-fade at each phase boundary. Mutually-prime durations so it never syncs with scroll

motion.name: breath-label
motion.value: 320ms ease-tea cross-fade, opacity only — the romanized beat (吸う · hold · 吐く) changes under the cue as the phase turns

motion.name: scrub-lerp
motion.value: v += (t − v)(1 − 0.88^(dt/16.7)) — dt-normalized half-life ≈ 5.4 frames; the chasen never jumps even on a 120Hz display

motion.name: scrub-window
motion.value: start: section center at 62vh; span: min(0.9·height, 1.2·vh) — the whisk draws only while its chashitsu is the room you are in

motion.name: chasen-draw
motion.value: pathLength=1 paths, stroke-dashoffset: calc(1 − var(--ch-scrub, 1)); 80 bamboo tines fan from a single node as scroll progresses, culminating in the bowl's matcha surface (opacity calc(scrub·5 − 4))

motion.name: reveal
motion.value: 1100ms ease-tea, translateY(16px) → 0, 140ms stagger per observer batch — slower than the gallery default, because the ceremony asks you to slow down

motion.name: tategaki-rise
motion.value: 1300ms ease-tea per glyph, vertical, translateY along the column at 220ms stagger — the header writes itself top-to-bottom, the only direction a brush ever travels

motion.name: ambient
motion.value: bowl steam hint 7.2s (opacity/transform only), breath cue 14s — mutually prime; everything else is still
